cards.title: North Pole (Negative) — Sedating
cards._legacyHtml:
Properties: Cooling, sedating, anti-inflammatory, anti-infective, analgesic. Inhibits bacterial growth. Reduces pain and inflammation.
Indications: Acute pain, inflammation, infection, fever, hypertension, insomnia, anxiety, tachycardia, toothache, headache, arthritis (acute), cancer pain (adjunct), wounds.
Method: Apply North pole face (marked N or with flat surface) directly to the affected area. Use for 15–30 min/session.
Mechanism: North pole creates alkaline environment, inhibits pathogens, reduces oedema, stimulates parasympathetic system.
Precaution: Never apply both poles simultaneously to the same area. Do not use over pacemakers, implants, or pregnancy abdomen.

cards.title: South Pole (Positive) — Stimulating
cards._legacyHtml:
Properties: Warming, stimulating, promotes growth, increases circulation. Creates acidic microenvironment.
Indications: Paralysis, weakness, hypothyroidism, chronic fatigue, poor circulation, delayed wound healing (after acute phase), degenerative conditions, low BP, cold limbs.
Method: Apply South pole face to affected area for 15–20 min. Use sparingly — excess stimulation can worsen acute conditions.
Mechanism: South pole increases metabolic activity, promotes cell proliferation, stimulates sympathetic system, increases acid production in tissue.
Contraindications: Acute infection, fever, acute inflammation, hypertension, malignancy (avoid direct application).

cards.title: Static Magnet — Types & Strengths
cards._legacyHtml:
Ceramic (ferrite) magnets: 500–3,000 Gauss. Most commonly used in naturopathy clinics. Cost-effective. For local application.
Neodymium (rare earth) magnets: 3,000–12,000 Gauss. Stronger, smaller. Used for deep tissue and acupoints.
Flexible magnetic strips: 100–500 Gauss. For wrapping limbs, back belts. Good for chronic pain management.
Magnetic insoles: 800–1,000 Gauss. For plantar fasciitis, fatigue, diabetic neuropathy (feet).
Magnetic bracelets/wristbands: 300–800 Gauss. For arthritis, carpal tunnel (limited evidence).
Clinical rule: 1 Gauss = 0.1 mT. Higher Gauss = shorter session needed. Document polarity used for each application.

cards.title: Magnetic Water — Preparation & Dosage
cards._legacyHtml:
Preparation: Place a 1,000–3,000 Gauss magnet (North pole facing water) under or beside a glass/copper vessel of water for 12–24 hours. Water becomes magnetised — altered hydrogen bonding, reduced surface tension, changed pH.
North pole water (alkaline): Anti-inflammatory, anti-infective. Dose: 100–150 ml, 2–3 times/day before meals. For: arthritis, infection, inflammation, constipation.
South pole water (acidic): Stimulating, digestive. Dose: 50–100 ml before meals. For: weak digestion, fatigue, low BP.
Storage: Use within 24 hours. Store in glass container away from direct sunlight and EMF sources.
Note: Evidence is preliminary; use as adjunct, not primary therapy.

cards.title: PEMF — Pulsed Electromagnetic Field Therapy
cards._legacyHtml:
Principle: Time-varying magnetic fields induce weak electric currents in tissue, stimulating cellular repair.
Frequencies used: 1–100 Hz (ELF-PEMF). Common protocols: 10 Hz (bone healing), 50–100 Hz (pain, muscle).
Intensities: 1–100 mT (clinical devices) to 0.1–1 mT (home devices).
Evidence-based indications: Non-union fractures (FDA-approved), osteoarthritis (Level 2A), chronic low back pain, depression (rTMS variant, Level 1A for TRD), fibromyalgia.
Session: 20–60 min, 1–2 times/day. Course: 4–6 weeks minimum.
Contraindications: Pacemaker, cochlear implants, pregnancy, active bleeding, seizure disorder, metallic implants at treatment site.

cards.title: Magneto Therapy — Indications by System
cards._legacyHtml:
Musculoskeletal: Rheumatoid arthritis (N pole), osteoarthritis (N pole acute, S pole chronic), fracture healing (S pole), tendinitis (N pole), LBP (N pole local).
Neurological: Sciatica (N pole), peripheral neuropathy (N pole), migraine (N pole temples), insomnia (N pole head/neck).
Cardiovascular: Hypertension (N pole cardiac region), varicose veins (N pole locally).
Endocrine: Hypothyroidism (S pole thyroid region), diabetes (N pole pancreatic region).
Gastrointestinal: Constipation (S pole abdominal), IBS (N pole abdominal), acidity (N pole epigastric).
Skin: Wounds (N pole), eczema (N pole), fungal infections (N pole).
ENT: Sinusitis (N pole), toothache (N pole), tonsillitis (N pole throat).

cards.title: Magneto Therapy — Safety & Contraindications
cards._legacyHtml:
Absolute contraindications:
• Cardiac pacemaker or implanted defibrillator
• Cochlear implants or metallic middle ear implants
• Pregnancy (avoid abdomen/pelvis)
• Active haemorrhage or bleeding disorders
• Metallic implants at or near treatment site

Relative contraindications:
• Epilepsy (use with caution)
• Hyperthyroidism (avoid thyroid area)
• Malignancy (direct application over tumour site)
• Skin wounds or infections (South pole only — prefer North pole)

General safety rules:
• Store magnets away from credit cards, watches, electronic devices
• Do not apply to eyes directly
• Keep session to 30 min maximum for static magnets
• Remove watches and metal jewellery before application
• Pregnant healthcare workers should avoid handling strong magnets
